name: La Peridiella Trail
name_original: Senda La Cueva La Peridiella — circular desde Infiesto
description: Hike the La Peridiella Trail, a 6.6-mile moderate route through magical forests and along the Marea River, starting at a unique cave sanctua
region_intro: Hiking around Piloña, located in Asturias, Spain, offers diverse and well-preserved natural environments. The region features a rich landscape of mountains, valleys, and extensive forests, providing varied terrain for exploration. Hikers can experience lush mountain ranges, picturesque valleys like Borines, and significant natural landmarks such as the Sierra del Sueve. The area is also characterized by numerous rivers and waterfalls, including the Cascada del Chorrón and the scenic Río del Infierno. Best hiking trails around Piloña The most popular hiking route is Villamayor - El Chorrón - La Cueva Trail, a 6.4 miles (10.3 km) trail that takes 3 hours 29 minutes to complete. This route leads to the impressive Cascada del Chorrón and an abandoned village. Another top favourite among local hikers is La Peridiella Trail, a moderate 6.6 miles (10.7 km) path. It winds through magical forests and along the Marea River, starting near a unique cave sanctuary. Local hikers also love the Foz de los Moñacos loop from La Pesanca, an 8.1 miles (13.1 km) trail leading through the Foz de los Moñacos gorge, often completed in about 4 hours 34 minutes. Hiking around Piloña is defined by its lush mountain ranges, deep valleys, and extensive beech forests. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes, catering to various ability levels. The routes in Piloña are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 500 reviews. More than 3,900 hikers have used komoot to explore Piloña's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Piloña?
A: Piloña offers a diverse network of over 18 hiking trails. These routes cater to various skill levels, including easy, moderate, and difficult options, ensuring there's something for every hiker.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking in Piloña?
A: Piloña is characterized by its rich natural environment, featuring lush mountain ranges, picturesque valleys like Borines, and extensive forests dominated by beech, eucalyptus, and alder trees. You'll also encounter numerous rivers, such as the scenic Río del Infierno, and impressive waterfalls like the Cascada del Chorrón.

Q: Are there any trails in Piloña that feature waterfalls?
A: Yes, the region is home to the impressive Cascada del Chorrón. You can experience this 15-meter waterfall by hiking the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1048082284/villamayor-el-chorron-la-cueva-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Villamayor - El Chorrón - La Cueva Trail&lt;/a&gt;, which leads directly to it.

Q: Are there any easy hiking trails suitable for beginners in Piloña?
A: Piloña has several easy routes perfect for beginners. One such option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10505890" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chorrón Waterfall – Villamayor loop from Villamayor&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 5.3 km trail with minimal elevation gain, offering a pleasant introduction to the area's beauty.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes in Piloña?
A: Yes, Piloña offers several excellent circular routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38174045" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Foz de los Moñacos loop from La Pesanca&lt;/a&gt; is a popular moderate 13.1 km option, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10505890" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chorrón Waterfall – Villamayor loop from Villamayor&lt;/a&gt; provides a shorter, easier circular experience.

Q: Where can I find trails with panoramic viewpoints in Piloña?
A: For breathtaking panoramic views, consider routes that ascend to higher elevations. The ascent to Monte Cayón is highly recommended, offering vistas that include the mountains of Lodeña and Incós, the valleys of Ques and Pintueles, the Sierra del Sueve, and even the distant Picos de Europa. While not a specific komoot route, many trails in the region will offer glimpses of these stunning landscapes.

Q: Are there opportunities for wild swimming along hiking routes in Piloña?
A: Yes, the Cascada del Chorrón forms a natural pool that is popular for swimming during the summer months. Additionally, the Río del Infierno, with its refreshing waters, offers spots where you can cool off after a hike, particularly in the high meadow of Los Moñacos.

Q: What are some longer day hike options for experienced hikers in Piloña?
A: For experienced hikers seeking a challenge, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40311645" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Foz de los Moñacos – Majada de la Felguerina loop from La Pesanca&lt;/a&gt; is a demanding 17.0 km route with a substantial elevation gain, offering a rewarding full-day adventure.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy the most about hiking in Piloña?
A: The trails in Piloña are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 580 reviews. Hikers often praise the region's untouched natural environment, the diversity of trails through forests, along rivers, and up mountains, and the well-preserved landscapes.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking options in Piloña?
A: Many of Piloña's trails are suitable for families, especially those looking for a moderate outing. Shorter sections of routes or trails with less elevation gain, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10505890" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chorrón Waterfall – Villamayor loop from Villamayor&lt;/a&gt;, are great choices for families to enjoy the natural beauty together.

Q: What interesting historical or cultural sites can I explore near the hiking trails?
A: Piloña is rich in history and culture. You can visit the 16th-century Sanctuary of the Virgin of the Cave, nestled under a large rock. The region also features traditional Asturian villages like Espinaréu, known for its *hórreos* (traditional granaries), and Villamayor, with its historical sites like the old Monastery of Santa María.

Q: Are there any mountain peaks or passes to explore around Piloña?
A: Yes, the area around Piloña offers several notable peaks and mountain passes. You can explore highlights such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1029177" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pienzu Peak&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5599992" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Les Praeres – Sierra de Peñamayor&lt;/a&gt;, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5544322" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Collada d'Arniciu&lt;/a&gt;, providing opportunities for more challenging ascents and stunning views.
tips: The sanctuary is from the 16th century and is located on the outskirts of Infiesto, next to the Marea River under a huge flat and upward-sloping rock. It is a beautiful place.
Beautiful place. The track is always close to the river, and it is very pleasant to walk with abundant vegetation.

The Senda de la Peridiella is a path full of charm and surprises, especially for children. On the path we will discover endless details of goblins, gnomes and even the Little Mouse Pérez himself. The initiative to create this magical world in La Peridiella has come from Gely Cobián, a neighbor of Infiesto.

faqs.question: What is the overall difficulty of the La Peridiella Trail?
faqs.answer: The main linear route of the La Peridiella Trail is generally considered easy, featuring largely flat terrain and a firm underfoot, making it suitable for families with young children and even strollers. However, if you opt for the circular extension that includes an ascent towards "La Llana," that specific section introduces a small slope and is rated as moderate, which might not be ideal for very small children.

faqs.question: What unique features or landmarks can I expect to see along the route?
faqs.answer: This trail is renowned for its "magical" atmosphere, created by artist Ángeles Cobián. You'll encounter mythological and fantastic figures, decorated doors, and charming messages, including the famous "Ratoncito Pérez" (Little Tooth Mouse) house. The route also begins at the unique Sanctuary of the Virgin of the Cave, which is built directly into a cave. Further along, you'll pass the Hermitage of the Virgin of the Rock and La Marea River.

faqs.question: Where does the trail begin, and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The trail typically begins at the Sanctuary of the Virgin of the Cave in Piloña, Asturias. Parking is usually available near this starting point, though it's always a good idea to arrive early during peak seasons to secure a spot.

faqs.question: Is the La Peridiella Trail suitable for families with young children or strollers?
faqs.answer: Yes, the main linear path of the trail is very suitable for families with young children and is even accessible with strollers due to its gentle, largely flat terrain and firm surface. However, the optional circular extension that climbs towards "La Llana" is steeper and not recommended for small children or strollers.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this hike?
faqs.answer: The terrain is largely flat ("llana") with minimal elevation change. The path offers a very good, firm underfoot, consisting of sections of earth, cobblestone, and even some asphalt. It predominantly follows the picturesque banks of the Marea River.

faqs.question: Is the trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the trail is generally welcoming to dogs, it's always best to keep your canine companion on a leash to ensure their safety and respect for other hikers and local wildlife. Please also remember to clean up after your pet.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike the La Peridiella Trail?
faqs.answer: The trail can be enjoyed year-round, but spring and autumn often offer the most pleasant hiking conditions with mild temperatures and vibrant natural scenery. Summer is also popular, though it can be warmer. The lush forests and river views are beautiful in any season.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required to access the trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no permits or entrance fees required to hike the La Peridiella Trail. It is freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: Does the route intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route also passes through sections of the Camín a La Peridiella, Camín de La Peridiella, Camín de La Llana a La Cueva, and Camín a Ques.

faqs.question: Are there facilities like restrooms or places to eat along the way?
faqs.answer: You'll find recreational areas and bars available at the beginning of the trail, near the Sanctuary of the Virgin of the Cave. Sometimes, additional facilities may be available closer to the end of the route, offering convenient spots for refreshments.

faqs.question: Is the La Peridiella Trail a loop or an out-and-back route?
faqs.answer: The trail offers both options. The primary route is often enjoyed as a linear path, but there is an option for a circular extension that includes a climb towards "La Llana," allowing for a loop experience.

waypoints.name: Sanctuary of the Virgin of the Cave
Hermitage of the Virgin of the Rock and La Marea River
